# 🔬 Science (149)

AI is rewriting the pace of discovery — folding proteins in hours, simulating particles, and proving theorems. These repos put research-grade models in your hands, the same tooling pushing the frontier in labs worldwide.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## Computational Biology

Genomics pipelines, protein structure predictors, and sequence models decoding life itself

<details>
<summary>Show 25 repos</summary>

- [google-deepmind/alphafold3](https://github.com/google-deepmind/alphafold3) — ⭐ 8,160 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the inference pipeline for AlphaFold 3, allowing users to run the model to predict protein structures. It serves as the open-source implementation for applying the AlphaFold 3 scientific breakthrough.
- [Biohub/esm](https://github.com/Biohub/esm) — ⭐ 2,394 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository is related to ESM (Evolutionary Scale Modeling) for proteins, likely providing code and examples for protein language models and computational biology research. It uses Jupyter Notebooks for analysis and demonstrations.
- [aipoch/medical-research-skills](https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills) — ⭐ 1,015 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This repository provides over 550 AI agent skills designed for various medical research tasks, including protocol design, data analysis, evidence synthesis, and academic writing.
- [NVIDIA/cuEquivariance](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cuEquivariance) — ⭐ 408 · `Python` · Library — This math library provides low-level primitives and tensor operations to accelerate equivariant neural networks and structure prediction. It is designed for use with widely-used models like DiffDock, MACE, Allegro, and NEQUIP in scientific computing.
- [NVIDIA-BioNeMo/bionemo-agent-toolkit](https://github.com/NVIDIA-BioNeMo/bionemo-agent-toolkit) — ⭐ 352 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This toolkit allows general AI agents to become experts in life sciences by integrating NVIDIA BioNeMo skills. It enables agents to specialize in biological and pharmaceutical applications.
- [Zyphra/zuna](https://github.com/Zyphra/zuna) — ⭐ 304 · `Python` · Library — A Python package providing the Zuna foundation model for inferring insights from EEG (Electroencephalography) data. It's designed to handle variable-length, real-world EEG recordings, overcoming limitations of models trained on clean, fixed-length data.
- [jaechang-hits/SciAgent-Skills](https://github.com/jaechang-hits/SciAgent-Skills) — ⭐ 239 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This repository offers a collection of 197 bioinformatics and life science skills specifically designed for Claude Code and other AI agents. It enables agents to perform tasks related to RNA-seq, single-cell analysis, drug discovery, and proteomics, significantly improving performance in scientific benchmarks.
- [exon-research/genomi](https://github.com/exon-research/genomi) — ⭐ 216 · `Python` · Agent Framework — This is an open-source agent harness that allows an AI agent to analyze personal genomic data locally. It builds a local index of DNA variations and connects to public databases like ClinVar, gnomAD, and PharmGKB, enabling the agent to answer questions about drug metabolism, genetic risks, and nutrition.
- [steineggerlab/folddisco](https://github.com/steineggerlab/folddisco) — ⭐ 161 · `Rust` · Library — Folddisco is a fast structural motif search engine designed to index and query millions of clustered AlphaFoldDB protein structures, enabling large-scale discovery of short motifs in seconds.
- [Wublab/Pythia](https://github.com/Wublab/Pythia) — ⭐ 104 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides a model that leverages structure-based self-supervised learning for ultrafast prediction of protein stability changes upon mutations. It is a core component of a broader protein engineering platform.
- [moritztng/tt-bio](https://github.com/moritztng/tt-bio) — ⭐ 96 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides Python implementations of biomolecular models, including Boltz-2, BoltzGen, and ESMFold-2. It is specifically optimized for inference on Tenstorrent hardware.
- [MLNeurosurg/neurovfm](https://github.com/MLNeurosurg/neurovfm) — ⭐ 40 · `Python` · Paper Code — NeuroVFM is a generalist neuroimaging foundation model, specifically Vol-JEPA, designed to process and learn from large datasets of volumetric medical images like MRI and CT scans for various health system applications.
- [Wublab/pythia\_ppi](https://github.com/Wublab/pythia_ppi) — ⭐ 35 · `Python` · Library — This is a specialized version of the Pythia model designed to predict changes in protein-protein interaction (PPI) affinity following mutations. It's part of a comprehensive protein engineering suite.
- [PDBeurope/SIFTS](https://github.com/PDBeurope/SIFTS) — ⭐ 30 · `Python` · Library — PDBe-SIFTS is an open-source tool developed with PDBeurope and UniProt for accurately mapping residue-level information between protein sequences and structures. It aids in bioinformatics research by ensuring high agreement with curated biological data.
- [yehlincho/absolute-stability-predictor](https://github.com/yehlincho/absolute-stability-predictor) — ⭐ 26 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository contains code and models for accurately predicting the absolute stability of small protein domains. It leverages mega-scale experimental data and PLM-based models (SaProtΔG·ESM3ΔG) to significantly improve prediction accuracy for domains under 100 residues.
- [AllenNeuralDynamics/openscope-community-predictive-processing](https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/openscope-community-predictive-processing) — ⭐ 23 · `Python` · Dataset — This repository serves as a community hub for the OpenScope Predictive Processing project, providing a central location for contributions, documentation, and project tracking related to neuronal recordings and visual prediction tasks, with public data available for analysis.
- [gelnesr/dEVA](https://github.com/gelnesr/dEVA) — ⭐ 21 · `Python` · Paper Code — A tool that implements a multi-objective evolutionary algorithm (dEVA) for the zero-shot design of de novo metalloenzymes, capable of creating enzymes like dizinc hydrolases with catalytic efficiencies comparable to natural phosphatases without natural templates.
- [PickyBinders/tea](https://github.com/PickyBinders/tea) — ⭐ 20 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project rewrites protein alphabets using language models, converting protein language model embeddings into a new 20-character "TEA" alphabet via contrastive learning. It enables distant homology detection comparable to structure-based methods.
- [haddocking/DeepRank-Ab](https://github.com/haddocking/DeepRank-Ab) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Library — This repository contains DeepRank-Ab, an AI model and associated software designed to score antibody-antigen complexes, improving the ranking of protein interaction poses.
- [bjzgcai/FlexRibbon](https://github.com/bjzgcai/FlexRibbon) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Paper Code — FlexRibbon is a pre-trained protein model that jointly learns from amino acid sequences and 3D structures. It aims to capture flexible protein conformations, useful for advanced protein modeling.
- [kundajelab/regulatory\_lm](https://github.com/kundajelab/regulatory_lm) — ⭐ 16 · `Python` · Paper Code — This is the official repository for ARSENAL, a masked DNA language model. It is pretrained specifically on regulatory DNA to learn transferable representations for computational biology tasks.
- [hongzhonglu/AlphaGEM](https://github.com/hongzhonglu/AlphaGEM) — ⭐ 15 · `Python` · Library — AlphaGEM is an automated toolbox that enables precise genome-scale metabolic modeling for non-model organisms. It integrates protein structure alignment with deep-learning-based dark metabolism mining to build ready-to-use genome-scale metabolic models.
- [ben1ahrens/Claude-Science-Windows](https://github.com/ben1ahrens/Claude-Science-Windows) — ⭐ 14 · `Shell` · CLI Tool — This is an installer and taskbar launcher that allows users to run Anthropic's Claude Science research application as a native desktop app on Windows, leveraging a WSL2 backend.
- [bjing2016/promera](https://github.com/bjing2016/promera) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository appears to be associated with the 'Promera' project, likely containing code or data that supports a scientific research paper published on BioRxiv, indicating a focus on computational biology or a related scientific discipline.
- [yang-ai-lab/Inertia-1](https://github.com/yang-ai-lab/Inertia-1) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project provides an open framework and "cookbook" for developing unified foundation models based on wearable motion data, such as from accelerometers. It's intended for exploring and building AI models for applications like healthcare and activity monitoring.

</details>

## Drug Discovery

Molecular generators and binding predictors compressing years of wet-lab work into compute

- [NVIDIA-BioNeMo/bionemo-recipes](https://github.com/NVIDIA-BioNeMo/bionemo-recipes) — ⭐ 823 · `Python` · Boilerplate/Template — This repository provides BioNeMo Recipes, which are resources and templates for efficiently building and adapting AI models specifically tailored for drug discovery applications.
- [aurekaresearch/OpenDDE](https://github.com/aurekaresearch/OpenDDE) — ⭐ 292 · `Python` · Framework — This repository presents OpenDDE, an open-source drug discovery engine that provides an all-atom model for predicting multi-component complexes with high co-folding accuracy within a reproducible framework.
- [THUNLP-MT/PepGLAD](https://github.com/THUNLP-MT/PepGLAD) — ⭐ 114 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code for a research paper on full-atom peptide design using geometric latent diffusion. It enables the generation of peptides with specified atomic structures, contributing to areas like drug discovery.
- [NVIDIA-BioNeMo/KERMT](https://github.com/NVIDIA-BioNeMo/KERMT) — ⭐ 78 · `Python` · Model Weights — KERMT is a pretrained graph neural network model for molecular property prediction, providing a 70M-parameter checkpoint and training code. It's designed for scientific applications like ADMET prediction in drug discovery.
- [recursionpharma/nesso](https://github.com/recursionpharma/nesso) — ⭐ 28 · `Python` · Framework — Nesso-1 is an open-source, coarse-grained cofolding framework designed to accelerate protein-ligand binding affinity predictions. It can predict binding in approximately one second on a single GPU, making it significantly faster than existing tools.
- [smiles724/Proteo-R1](https://github.com/smiles724/Proteo-R1) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · Paper Code — Proteo-R1 is a foundation model for protein reasoning, designed for drug discovery. It focuses on identifying critical protein residues to inform protein design, rather than just generating structures, aligning with human protein engineering practices.

## Physics

Neural simulators and surrogate models for dynamics that classical solvers choke on

- [openrocket/openrocket](https://github.com/openrocket/openrocket) — ⭐ 2,805 · `Java` · Application — OpenRocket is a software application for simulating the aerodynamics and trajectory of model rockets, allowing users to design, analyze, and optimize their rocket models.
- [optiland/optiland](https://github.com/optiland/optiland) — ⭐ 701 · `Python` · Library — Optiland is a Python library for comprehensive optical design, optimization, and analysis, featuring GPU-accelerated and differentiable ray tracing via PyTorch. It enables scientists and engineers to model and simulate complex optical systems.
- [FloatingPragma/observer-patch-holography](https://github.com/FloatingPragma/observer-patch-holography) — ⭐ 537 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents the Observer Patch Holography (OPH) theory, a simulation theory proposing that physics emerges from observer-consistent local patches. It includes an attempt to prove OPH in 10 pages, offering structural and numerical outputs with falsifiable predictions for scientific research.
- [CasberryIndia/Physics-Notebook](https://github.com/CasberryIndia/Physics-Notebook) — ⭐ 253 · `HTML` · Tutorial/Course — This repository offers an interactive collection of physics concepts, featuring beautiful visualizations and real-time simulations to help understand mathematical and physical principles through animations.
- [EA1FUO/AntennaSim](https://github.com/EA1FUO/AntennaSim) — ⭐ 248 · `TypeScript` · Application — A web-based antenna simulator powered by NEC2, allowing users to design, simulate, and analyze antennas. It provides real-time 3D visualization of radiation patterns, SWR, and impedance charts.
- [Sim2Reason/Sim2Reason](https://github.com/Sim2Reason/Sim2Reason) — ⭐ 150 · `Python` · Paper Code — Sim2Reason is a method that uses reinforcement learning on physics simulators to solve Physics Olympiad problems. It generates question-answer pairs to improve the physical reasoning of LLMs.

## Chemistry

Reaction predictors, retrosynthesis planners, and models that dream up new molecules

- [allenai/molmoact2](https://github.com/allenai/molmoact2) — ⭐ 151 · Framework — MolmoAct2 is a flexible framework for deep generative molecular design. It offers tools and methods to create and optimize novel molecules using advanced deep learning models.
- [AtomFlow-AI/MoleCode](https://github.com/AtomFlow-AI/MoleCode) — ⭐ 23 · `Python` · Library — A system that translates chemical molecules into a structured code format, allowing Large Language Models (LLMs) to directly process and reason about complex chemical information, significantly improving accuracy and reducing computational cost.

## Math

Theorem provers, symbolic solvers, and reasoning engines tackling formal proofs

<details>
<summary>Show 11 repos</summary>

- [fengdu78/Coursera-ML-AndrewNg-Notes](https://github.com/fengdu78/Coursera-ML-AndrewNg-Notes) — ⭐ 37,088 · `HTML` · Tutorial/Course — This repository contains comprehensive personal notes for Andrew Ng's popular Machine Learning course on Coursera, written in Chinese. It serves as a valuable resource to help individuals understand and master the foundational concepts of AI and machine learning.
- [Z3Prover/z3](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3) — ⭐ 12,325 · `C++` · Library — Z3 is a powerful theorem prover used for formal verification, program analysis, and solving complex logical constraints. Recent enhancements include agentic capabilities, allowing it to be integrated into AI systems for advanced reasoning.
- [jihe520/MathModelAgent](https://github.com/jihe520/MathModelAgent) — ⭐ 1,910 · `Vue` · AI Agent — An AI agent designed for mathematical modeling, capable of automatically solving math problems, generating code, and writing a complete, submission-ready paper. It includes a built-in code execution environment and supports multi-agent collaboration.
- [thieu1995/mealpy](https://github.com/thieu1995/mealpy) — ⭐ 1,199 · `Python` · Library — Mealpy is a comprehensive Python library offering over 230 state-of-the-art metaheuristic optimization algorithms, such as genetic algorithms and particle swarm optimization, applicable to various scientific and engineering problems.
- [yuanzhongqiao/deepmlab](https://github.com/yuanzhongqiao/deepmlab) — ⭐ 1,060 · `Scilab` · Application — This project is an open-source, LLM-driven alternative to MATLAB, enabling users to perform mathematical modeling and simulation using natural language. It automatically generates Scilab scripts for tasks like numerical analysis, signal processing, and control systems.
- [google-deepmind/formal-conjectures](https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures) — ⭐ 980 · `Lean` · Dataset — This repository provides a collection of mathematical conjectures that have been formally stated and verified within the Lean proof assistant. It acts as a specialized dataset for research in formal mathematics, potentially for use with AI-assisted theorem proving systems.
- [usail-hkust/LLM-MM-Agent](https://github.com/usail-hkust/LLM-MM-Agent) — ⭐ 532 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository presents MM-Agent, a system that utilizes LLM agents to tackle real-world mathematical modeling problems, specifically highlighted for a NeurIPS 2025 publication.
- [thuml/Transolver](https://github.com/thuml/Transolver) — ⭐ 350 · `Python` · Paper Code — Transolver is a Python library that implements a fast Transformer-based solver for Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) on complex geometries. It's designed for efficient scientific computing, particularly for large-scale problems described in an ICML 2024 paper.
- [KeyAI/calculusmadeeasy-zh](https://github.com/KeyAI/calculusmadeeasy-zh) — ⭐ 216 · `HTML` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides an unofficial Simplified Chinese translation of Silvanus P. Thompson's classic textbook, "Calculus Made Easy." It includes HTML versions of both the original English and the translated Chinese text, along with tools for translation validation, making the educational content accessible to Chinese readers.
- [disco-lang/disco](https://github.com/disco-lang/disco) — ⭐ 182 · `TeX` · Library — Disco is a functional programming language designed for teaching discrete mathematics, emphasizing formal methods and clear logical expression.
- [Dao-AILab/gram-newton-schulz](https://github.com/Dao-AILab/gram-newton-schulz) — ⭐ 150 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository implements a fast polar decomposition algorithm, which is described as an optimization technique for 'Muon' and appears relevant for improving performance on GPU instances for deep learning models.

</details>

## Research Tools

Paper search, literature synthesis, and experiment trackers built for serious science

<details>
<summary>Show 89 repos</summary>

- [tinygrad/tinygrad](https://github.com/tinygrad/tinygrad) — ⭐ 33,066 · `Python` · Framework — Tinygrad is a minimalistic deep learning framework intended as a simpler, more accessible alternative to PyTorch and micrograd, ideal for learning the core mechanics of AI by reading code.
- [eriklindernoren/ML-From-Scratch](https://github.com/eriklindernoren/ML-From-Scratch) — ⭐ 31,470 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides bare-bones NumPy implementations of fundamental machine learning models and algorithms, ranging from linear regression to deep learning. It aims to teach machine learning concepts from scratch with a focus on accessibility.
- [shap/shap](https://github.com/shap/shap) — ⭐ 25,411 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Library — This library provides a game theoretic approach, specifically SHAP values, to explain the output of any machine learning model, enhancing interpretability.
- [K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills) — ⭐ 22,567 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This repository provides a collection of pre-built agent skills designed to empower AI models for various scientific, research, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing tasks. It offers 133 specialized skills across fields like bioinformatics, drug discovery, and materials science, particularly for Claude and Claude Code.
- [joonspk-research/generative\_agents](https://github.com/joonspk-research/generative_agents) — ⭐ 21,397 · Paper Code — This repository contains the code for "Generative Agents," AI entities designed to simulate human behavior and social interactions within a virtual town environment. It is used for research into the realistic modeling of AI agent autonomy and their daily routines.
- [afshinea/stanford-cs-229-machine-learning](https://github.com/afshinea/stanford-cs-229-machine-learning) — ⭐ 19,417 · Tutorial/Course — A collection of VIP cheatsheets for Stanford's CS 229 Machine Learning course, providing summaries of key concepts and refreshers on foundational math like probability and calculus.
- [karpathy/micrograd](https://github.com/karpathy/micrograd) — ⭐ 16,272 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Library — This repository provides a concise scalar-valued autograd engine and a neural network library with a PyTorch-like API, serving as an educational tool for understanding AI fundamentals through code.
- [microsoft/RD-Agent](https://github.com/microsoft/RD-Agent) — ⭐ 13,939 · `Python` · AI Agent — This project, RD-Agent, aims to automate generic research and development processes, especially in the AI era, by enabling AI to drive data-driven AI. It focuses on enhancing industrial productivity through the automation of data and model workflows.
- [SakanaAI/AI-Scientist](https://github.com/SakanaAI/AI-Scientist) — ⭐ 13,630 · `Jupyter Notebook` · AI Agent — This repository provides an AI Scientist system that utilizes autonomous LLM agents to conduct open-ended scientific discovery and generate scientific papers. It aims to achieve fully automated scientific research.
- [aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw](https://github.com/aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw) — ⭐ 12,370 · `Python` · AI Agent — AutoResearchClaw is an autonomous and self-evolving AI agent designed to generate scientific papers from an initial idea, leveraging specialized domain-expert agents for various scientific fields like physics and biology.
- [statsmodels/statsmodels](https://github.com/statsmodels/statsmodels) — ⭐ 11,406 · `Python` · Library — Statsmodels is a comprehensive Python library for statistical modeling and econometrics, offering tools for statistical inference, regression analysis, and time-series analysis, primarily used for academic research and hypothesis testing.
- [rushter/MLAlgorithms](https://github.com/rushter/MLAlgorithms) — ⭐ 11,008 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides minimal and clean Python implementations of various machine learning algorithms. It serves as a practical resource for understanding and demonstrating the core concepts of deep learning, neural networks, and other ML techniques.
- [cp-algorithms/cp-algorithms](https://github.com/cp-algorithms/cp-algorithms) — ⭐ 10,597 · `C++` · Tutorial/Course — This repository hosts a collection of articles and C++ implementations of algorithms and data structures, primarily intended as learning materials for competitive programming. It serves as the foundation for the cp-algorithms.com website.
- [unit8co/darts](https://github.com/unit8co/darts) — ⭐ 9,381 · `Python` · Library — Darts is a Python library designed for user-friendly time series forecasting and anomaly detection, unifying classical and deep learning models.
- [SakanaAI/AI-Scientist-v2](https://github.com/SakanaAI/AI-Scientist-v2) — ⭐ 6,210 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository provides the AI Scientist-v2, an AI system designed to automate scientific discovery through an agentic tree search mechanism. It focuses on generating scientific papers and performing workshop-level scientific research autonomously.
- [ImagineAILab/ai-by-hand-excel](https://github.com/ImagineAILab/ai-by-hand-excel) — ⭐ 6,106 · Plugin/Extension — This repository implements deep learning models directly within Microsoft Excel, serving as a specialized tool for scientific computing or data analysis using AI in a spreadsheet environment.
- [SamuelSchmidgall/AgentLaboratory](https://github.com/SamuelSchmidgall/AgentLaboratory) — ⭐ 5,630 · `Python` · AI Agent — Agent Laboratory provides an end-to-end autonomous research workflow, leveraging specialized agents to help human researchers implement their ideas and automate full research processes.
- [louisfb01/start-machine-learning](https://github.com/louisfb01/start-machine-learning) — ⭐ 5,274 · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a comprehensive guide for individuals to start and improve in machine learning and artificial intelligence, even without prior background. It aims to educate users on core concepts and keep them updated with the latest advancements and techniques in the field.
- [sgoudelis/ground-station](https://github.com/sgoudelis/ground-station) — ⭐ 4,272 · `JavaScript` · Application — Monitors satellites, processes radio signals from SDRs, and displays related data for satellite tracking, signal analysis, and weather monitoring.
- [anthropics/original\_performance\_takehome](https://github.com/anthropics/original_performance_takehome) — ⭐ 4,043 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains Anthropic's original performance take-home challenge, now public for users to test and benchmark AI model capabilities, particularly referencing Claude Opus.
- [HenryNdubuaku/maths-cs-ai-compendium](https://github.com/HenryNdubuaku/maths-cs-ai-compendium) — ⭐ 3,796 · `TypeScript` · Tutorial/Course — A comprehensive compendium of 'notebook notes' designed to educate aspiring AI/ML Research Engineers. It covers essential topics across mathematics, computer science, and artificial intelligence, from foundational concepts to advanced deep learning, NLP, and multimodal learning.
- [Yuan1z0825/nature-skills](https://github.com/Yuan1z0825/nature-skills) — ⭐ 3,491 · `Python` · Skill Pack — An AI skill designed to conform to the academic expression and scientific plotting standards required for Nature papers.
- [ResearAI/AutoFigure-Edit](https://github.com/ResearAI/AutoFigure-Edit) — ⭐ 3,301 · `Python` · Application — AutoFigure-Edit is a tool that transforms scientific method text into editable SVG figures. It aims to help researchers visualize and modify their experimental procedures and diagrams.
- [pageman/sutskever-30-implementations](https://github.com/pageman/sutskever-30-implementations) — ⭐ 3,296 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This project provides pure NumPy implementations of 30 foundational AI papers recommended by Ilya Sutskever, designed for interactive learning via Jupyter Notebooks. It covers essential AI models like RNN, Transformer, and RAG from scratch to help users understand core concepts.
- [opengeos/geoai](https://github.com/opengeos/geoai) — ⭐ 3,002 · `Python` · Library — The GeoAI repository provides a Python package for applying artificial intelligence to geospatial data. It offers tools for researchers and developers to work with earth observation and remote sensing data, including tasks like segmentation.
- [ResearAI/DeepScientist](https://github.com/ResearAI/DeepScientist) — ⭐ 2,943 · `TypeScript` · AI Agent — A tool that runs autonomous research loops locally on a user's machine. It aims to push the frontiers of AI research.
- [EvoScientist/EvoScientist](https://github.com/EvoScientist/EvoScientist) — ⭐ 2,755 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository provides a system that harnesses self-evolving AI scientists to conduct research. It aims to leverage AI to drive scientific discovery.
- [blazickjp/arxiv-mcp-server](https://github.com/blazickjp/arxiv-mcp-server) — ⭐ 2,704 · `Python` · MCP Server — This is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that integrates LLMs, particularly Claude, with arXiv for academic literature review. It enables autonomous searching, downloading, reading, and analyzing of research papers by streamlining interactions with the arXiv API.
- [synthetic-sciences/openscience](https://github.com/synthetic-sciences/openscience) — ⭐ 2,343 · `TypeScript` · AI Agent — An open-source AI workbench designed for scientific research, it automates tasks such as literature review, code generation, experiment execution, and paper drafting. This tool acts as a "co-scientist" to accelerate scientific discovery.
- [ndrplz/ConvLSTM\_pytorch](https://github.com/ndrplz/ConvLSTM_pytorch) — ⭐ 2,254 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides a PyTorch implementation of a Convolutional LSTM (ConvLSTM) neural network. It's mentioned in the context of advanced bias correction for weather trading bots and large-scale weather prediction models.
- [Master-cai/Research-Paper-Writing-Skills](https://github.com/Master-cai/Research-Paper-Writing-Skills) — ⭐ 1,982 · Skill Pack — This repository offers a skill package for writing ML, CV, and NLP research papers, curated from Prof. Peng Sida's notes and adapted for use with AI coding assistants like Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini.
- [delibae/claude-prism](https://github.com/delibae/claude-prism) — ⭐ 1,492 · `TypeScript` · Application — An offline-first desktop application for scientific writing, combining a LaTeX editor with Python and over 100 scientific skills powered by Claude AI, all running locally.
- [llmsresearch/paperbanana](https://github.com/llmsresearch/paperbanana) — ⭐ 1,427 · `Python` · MCP Server — PaperBanana is an open-source tool based on Google Research's PaperBanana, designed to automate the generation of academic figures, diagrams, and research visuals, including slide generation. It uses VLMs to understand research logic and produce code for high-quality vector graphics.
- [lilipads/gradient\_descent\_viz](https://github.com/lilipads/gradient_descent_viz) — ⭐ 1,388 · `C++` · Application — An interactive visualization tool that illustrates five popular gradient descent methods step-by-step, featuring a user interface for hyperparameter tuning.
- [anthropics/jacobian-lens](https://github.com/anthropics/jacobian-lens) — ⭐ 1,344 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the companion code for a research paper on "global workspace interpretability," likely focusing on understanding and analyzing the internal workings of AI models.
- [maziyarpanahi/openmed](https://github.com/maziyarpanahi/openmed) — ⭐ 1,100 · `Python` · Library — OpenMed is an open-source project dedicated to healthcare AI, leveraging various large language models (LLMs) like BERT, Llama, and Qwen. It aims to develop and deploy on-device and on-premise AI solutions tailored for the healthcare domain.
- [aaronjmars/MiroShark](https://github.com/aaronjmars/MiroShark) — ⭐ 1,071 · `Python` · Application — MiroShark is a universal swarm intelligence engine designed for rapid and cost-effective simulation of various scenarios, including financial forecasting and future prediction, utilizing AI for diverse analytical tasks.
- [AllenDowney/ThinkStats](https://github.com/AllenDowney/ThinkStats) — ⭐ 887 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository contains Jupyter notebooks that serve as a practical, code-driven guide to learning statistics using Python. It focuses on explaining statistical concepts through data and programs, covering topics from exploratory data analysis to hypothesis testing and regression.
- [K-Dense-AI/k-dense-byok](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/k-dense-byok) — ⭐ 786 · `TypeScript` · AI Agent — This project is an open-source AI co-scientist designed to run on a user's desktop, utilizing a 'bring-your-own-key' approach for various LLMs, including local models. It comes equipped with over 135 scientific agent skills and numerous pre-built workflows to assist with scientific research, particularly in areas like drug discovery and bioinformatics.

## Other

Climate models, materials science, and cross-disciplinary gems too good to skip

- [microsoft/ML-For-Beginners](https://github.com/microsoft/ML-For-Beginners) — ⭐ 85,770 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a comprehensive 12-week curriculum with 26 lessons and 52 quizzes, designed to teach classic Machine Learning concepts to beginners.
- [ZuzooVn/machine-learning-for-software-engineers](https://github.com/ZuzooVn/machine-learning-for-software-engineers) — ⭐ 28,767 · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides a detailed, top-down daily study plan for software engineers aiming to transition into machine learning engineering roles. It outlines a complete curriculum to guide learners through the necessary concepts and skills in AI and deep learning.
- [ossu/data-science](https://github.com/ossu/data-science) — ⭐ 21,166 · Tutorial/Course — This repository offers a free, self-taught curriculum for data science, providing a structured, university-level educational path. It guides individuals through essential topics and resources for learning the field.
- [rt-bishop/Look4Sat](https://github.com/rt-bishop/Look4Sat) — ⭐ 1,100 · `Kotlin` · Application — This is an Android application for tracking over 9000 radio satellites and predicting their passes, drawing inspiration from Gpredict. It helps users monitor satellite movements and related information.
- [FrontierCS/Frontier-CS](https://github.com/FrontierCS/Frontier-CS) — ⭐ 194 · `C++` · Dataset — This repository provides a benchmark specifically designed for evaluating large language models (LLMs) on open-ended computer science problems. It aims to explore the next frontier of computer science by assessing LLM capabilities in complex problem-solving.
- [rkeisler/keisler-2022](https://github.com/rkeisler/keisler-2022) — ⭐ 76 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides a graph neural network model for weather forecasting, implementing techniques from Keisler 2022. It serves as a lightweight starting point for machine learning-based weather prediction.
- [xisen-w/hl-imagenet](https://github.com/xisen-w/hl-imagenet) — ⭐ 57 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository documents experiments in heuristic learning applied to ImageNet, focusing on a pure symbolic vision system that operates without neural networks or backpropagation. The development process itself was iteratively improved using AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Codex.
- [rudrankriyam/Google-Health-CLI](https://github.com/rudrankriyam/Google-Health-CLI) — ⭐ 52 · `Go` · CLI Tool — Unofficial Google-Health-CLI for the Google Health API, written in Go
- [FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin](https://github.com/FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a weather plugin for the Hermes Agent, providing high-quality meteorological data, including NWS-grade model imagery, NEXRAD radar, and verified calculations, implemented with a pure Rust stack.
- [ip-arch/NESLab](https://github.com/ip-arch/NESLab) — ⭐ 18 · `C++` · Tutorial/Course — NESLab is an educational environment that provides materials and exercises for learning embedded systems. It uses the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES) as a theme, allowing users to learn concepts like memory-mapped I/O and interrupts while interacting with an 8-bit game console, and supports an online emulator for experimentation.
